description:
You are a backup administrator auditing the size of the daily text logs stored under:

/home/user/data/backups/

This directory already contains three log files named:

• 2023-05-01.log  
• 2023-05-02.log  
• 2023-05-03.log  

Your job is to generate a one–off, line-count summary for these files and save it as:

/home/user/data/backup_reports/line_counts.csv

Output-format requirements (these will be checked by an automated test):

1. The file **/home/user/data/backup_reports/line_counts.csv** must be plain text.
2. It must contain exactly four lines, each terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF, 0x0A).  
3. Lines 1–3 list each existing “*.log” file **in ascending, lexicographical order by file name**.  
   • Each of those lines contains:  
     `<file-name>,<number-of-lines>`  
     with no extra spaces.  
4. Line 4 (the last one) must read:  
   `TOTAL,<sum-of-all-lines>`  
   where “<sum-of-all-lines>” is the arithmetic sum of the individual counts from the first three lines.  
5. No header row, no trailing blank lines and no additional columns are allowed.

Example of the exact required contents:

2023-05-01.log,5  
2023-05-02.log,3  
2023-05-03.log,4  
TOTAL,12

Goal: produce that CSV summary in one single compound terminal command (you may use pipes, redirections, subshells, etc.), without manually opening an editor. Make sure the command is executed from a location where the relative or absolute paths you reference resolve correctly.

When you are done, the file /home/user/data/backup_reports/line_counts.csv should exist and match the format above byte for byte. An automated test will read this file to verify success.
truth:
Initial filesystem state:

Directories:
1. /home/user/data/backups                (already exists, 755)
2. /home/user/data/backup_reports         (already exists, 755)

Files under /home/user/data/backups with exact contents:

/home/user/data/backups/2023-05-01.log  (permissions 644)
---------------------------------------------------------
2023-05-01 00:00:01 Backup job started
2023-05-01 00:15:23 Compressing data
2023-05-01 00:45:50 Uploading archive
2023-05-01 01:10:05 Verification successful
2023-05-01 01:10:06 Backup completed

/home/user/data/backups/2023-05-02.log  (permissions 644)
---------------------------------------------------------
2023-05-02 00:00:02 Backup job started
2023-05-02 00:54:10 Backup failed: network timeout
2023-05-02 00:54:11 Backup aborted

/home/user/data/backups/2023-05-03.log  (permissions 644)
---------------------------------------------------------
2023-05-03 00:00:02 Backup job started
2023-05-03 00:25:15 Compressing data
2023-05-03 00:49:40 Uploading archive
2023-05-03 01:05:12 Backup completed

Line counts (used by tester):
2023-05-01.log → 5
2023-05-02.log → 3
2023-05-03.log → 4
TOTAL          → 12

File that must be created by the agent:

/home/user/data/backup_reports/line_counts.csv  (permissions 644)
Expected exact content including final newline:

2023-05-01.log,5
2023-05-02.log,3
2023-05-03.log,4
TOTAL,12

The automated test will:
1. Confirm that the file exists at the correct path.
2. Verify byte-for-byte equality with the expected content above.
3. Ensure exactly four LF-terminated lines are present.
